content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Conflict of Interest ••• Administration ••• Executive/Legal ••• Enforcement ••• Conflict of Interest
(916) 323-5... 323-5690 323-5901 322-6441 322-5444

November 16, 1978

Jose Quesada
1757 Victor Boulevard
Arcata, CA 95521

Dear Mr. Quesada:

Thank you for your letter of October 17, 1978, seeking
advice concerning the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act. The facts as we understand them are
stated as follows:

You currently serve as a boardmember without pay for
the Manila Community Association. The Manila Community
Association is a non-profit entity whose membership consists
of citizens of Filipino descent. The Association publishes
a monthly newsletter and maintains a community park on land
it has leased from Humboldt County. The annual budget of
the Association is well below $1,000.

The Association wishes to build a multi-purpose sports
field and nature trail in the above-mentioned park. The
Association is seeking funds from CETA in order that it may
hire a work crew, and obtain some of the equipment necessary
to begin work on the park project.

All requests for funds are reviewed by a CETA board and
ranked according to priority. A prioritized list of fund
requests is sent to the County Board of Supervisors who
reviews the list and makes any changes it believes may be
warranted. CETA then may contract, in order of priority,
with those individuals whose projects have been approved.

Your letter indicates that in late April or early May
you were hired on a temporary basis by CETA as a summer
counselor for the CETA Summer Program for the Economically
Disadvantaged Youth. As a counselor, you were not involved
in reviewing CETA fund applications. It is our understanding
that your contract for employment with CETA has expired and
you are no longer in their employ.

Jose Quesada
November 16, 1978
Page Two


You have asked whether it would constitute a conflict
of interest under the Political Reform Act, if, in your
private capacity you were to assist the Manila Community
Association in applying for funds to construct the above-
mentioned park. Based on the information provided, it is
our conclusion that you do not have a conflict of interest
under to the Political Reform Act and may assist the Asso-
ciation with its proposal for funding from CETA.

Section 87100 of the Political Reform Act provides:

     No public official at any level of state or
     local government shall make, participate in making
     or in any way attempt to use his official position
     to influence a governmental decision in which he
     knows or has reason to know he has a financial
     interest.

Section 87103 states that:

     An official has a financial interest in a
     decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
     is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
     have a material financial effect, distinguishable
     from its effect on the public generally, on:

                        . . .

     (c)  Any source of income, other than loans
     by a commercial lending institution in the regular
     course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
     dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
     promised to the public official within twelve
     months prior to the time when the decision is
     made; or

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public
     official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
     employee, or holds any position of management.

     For purposes of this section, indirect invest-
     ment or interest means any investment or interest
     owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public
     official, by an agent on behalf of a public official,
     by any business entity controlled by the public
     official or by a trust in which he has a substantial

Jose Quesada
November 16, 1978
Page Three


                    interest. A business entity is controlled by a
public official if the public official, his agents,
spouse and dependent children hold more than fifty
percent of the ownership interest in the entity.
A public official has a substantial interest in a
trust when the official, his spouse and dependent
children have a present or future interest worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000).

     The Act requires a public official to disqualify himself
from participating in those decisions which may affect a
source of income from which he has received $250 or more.
The facts indicate that you do not receive any income from
the Manila Community Association.

     As mentioned previously, the Act requires you to dis-
qualify yourself from participating in decisions which may
foreseeably have a material affect on a business entity for
which you are director or board member. Section 82005 of
the Act defines business entity to mean:

          ... any organization or enterprise operated for
     profit, including but not limited to a proprietor-
     ship, partnership, firm, business trust, joint
     venture, syndicate, corporation or association.

     The Manila Community Association is a non-profit organization
with a budget of less than $1,000. Its activities are limited
to producing a newsletter and maintaining a community park.
Based on these facts, the Association does not appear to be
an entity within the meaning of the Act.

     Since the Manila Community Association is neither a
business entity or a source of income, you do not have a
financial interest in the Association. Accordingly the Act
does not prohibit you from participating in decisions affecting
the Association. If you have any questions regarding this
matter, please do not hesitate to contact our office con-
cerning this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Dwight E. Dickerson
                                        Staff Counsel
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:plh

October 17, 1978


ATTENTION: Office of Fair Political Practice
           Conflict of Interest/ Dwight Dickerson


Mr. Dickerson:

My name is Jose Quezada and I am a community elected board member
of the Manila Community Association, a non-profit organization
in our community of 1000 people between the cities of Arcata and
Eureka in Humboldt County. I serve on this neighborhood board
as Park-Community liaison without pay as do the other 6 members
of the board. One of the main objectives of the Association is the
development and upkeep of the park (Manila Park) thru community
fundraising efforts and the solicitation of public and industrial
donations.

In May of 1978 a CETA Title VI project proposal for the installation
of a multi-purpose sport field and nature trail for the park was
submitted to CETA for approval. It was written up by myself with
assistance from the Manila Community Association and concerned individual
in the community.

Two weeks prior to the proposal submittal I was hired by CETA to be
a summer counselor for the CETA Summer Program for the Economically
Disadvantaged Youth (SPEDY), a part-time temporary position to
